title: ophrys rosea subsp. neglecta
benefits: Ophrys rosea subsp. neglecta has many benefits, such as its ability to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that aid in the treatment of various conditions like arthritis and gout. This plant is also known to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which make it effective in wound healing and preventing infections. The antioxidants present in Ophrys rosea subsp. neglecta help protect against cell damage, reducing the risk of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. By incorporating this plant into one's daily life, individuals can experience improved overall health, enhanced cognitive function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a longer and more fulfilling life.
parts: Ophrys rosea subsp. neglecta has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and roots, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Ophrys rosea subsp. neglecta contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of the plant are rich in iridoid glycosides, including secologanin and secologanin glycosides, which are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The roots of Ophrys rosea subsp. neglecta contain saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
side_effects: Ophrys rosea subsp. neglecta can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. If ingested, it may cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. In some cases, excessive consumption may lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. Additionally, improper use of the plant may also cause interactions with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
